title: Native-audio value test suite
why_interesting: It attacks the central ambiguity in audio LLM research: what is gained by listening directly rather than reading transcripts?
why_useful: Could guide model choice, product design, and evaluation by isolating native audio advantages.
minimal_test: Collect a small public-safe set of tasks where transcript-only systems should lose information: prosody, turn-taking, speaker state, acoustic scene, and non-verbal cues.
next: Draft 10 candidate scenarios and map each to required audio information.

title: Codec-as-LLM-interface evaluation
why_interesting: Codec quality for reconstruction may not predict usefulness for audio LLM behavior.
why_useful: Could turn compression/tokenizer reading into a concrete research direction with clear experiments.
minimal_test: Compare public codec papers by frame rate, bitrate, causality, preserved semantic/prosodic information, and downstream task implications.
next: Start from EnCodec, Low Frame-rate Speech Codec, and Codec-SUPERB.

title: Spoken-agent failure mode taxonomy
why_interesting: The product value of audio LLMs may depend more on interaction failures than static benchmark scores.
why_useful: A taxonomy can turn vague UX pain into model/data/inference/eval research questions.
minimal_test: Write a public-safe list of 20 spoken-agent failures and classify root causes.
next: Add examples without using confidential product data.

title: Audio LLM paper decision rubric
why_interesting: A light rubric can prevent paper reading from becoming a passive collection habit.
why_useful: Keeps the system aligned with interesting/useful first and makes weekly review faster.
minimal_test: For each paper, score interesting/useful and assign one action: ignore, skim, read, deep read, reproduce, build on.
next: Apply to the first 9 dashboard papers.
